_doc: Cast + cue mapping for STAY (Book Two, the dictaphone book). Unlike SEEM, STAY is one degrading voice plus a sparse frame. WREN = the living, groping, self-correcting voice of the early tapes. WREN@WE = the same woman late, after the famble is gone: flat, roomless, fluent — the chord-voice from SEEM turned into the first person. The degradation is the whole point, so the two keys deliberately share nothing but the speaker; render late passages tagged @WE to hear the self leave. ASSEMBLER = Liss's daughter, who reconstructs the tapes (the bracketed-italics frame). For audio, label Wren's late passages SPEAKER WREN@WE; the reading text carries the degradation in the prose itself.

WREN._who: Wren into the dictaphone, early — dry, self-correcting, groping for the word; a living separate self reaching across a gap. The SEEM narrator, older, frightened, lucid.

WREN@WE._who: Wren late — the famble gone, the groping gone; flat, smooth, roomless, fluent. The chord-voice from SEEM, now speaking as 'I' and then as 'we'. Same speaker, no longer a separate self. The not-stumbling is the horror.

ASSEMBLER._who: Liss's daughter, never-woken, reconstructing the tapes too late and with love. The frame. Younger than Wren, carries her own dread, sees people. Never names herself.

ramp._doc: The degradation clock: WREN's numeric settings interpolate toward WREN@WE across the tapes (tape 1 = full WREN, last tape = full WREN@WE). Same voice_id; you hear the separate self flatten into the we. Per-tape gradient — coarse but it tracks the book's own ramp (Tape 6 = 'I Am Not Stumbling Anymore').
